parameters.jsCode:
// ---------------------------
// ENHANCED RAG DOCUMENTS WITH IMPROVED CATEGORIZATION
// ---------------------------

const documents = [

  // -------------------------
  // BRANDED TRACKING & DNS
  // -------------------------
  {
    pageContent: "BRANDED TRACKING COMPREHENSIVE GUIDE

What is Branded Tracking?
Branded tracking uses the client's own domain (e.g., visit.example.com) instead of Tradedoubler's generic tracking domains. This creates first-party tracking that browsers trust and don't block.

Key Benefits:
- 73% improvement in tracking accuracy with Safari ITP
- 16% better tracking than Google Analytics in Safari
- 11% increase in conversion rates
- Significantly less likely to be blocked by ad blockers
- Better SEO and brand consistency
- Full compliance with privacy regulations

How It Works:
Two subdomains are created:
1. visit.[yourdomain] - for click tracking/redirects
2. statics.[yourdomain] - for serving tracking scripts

Both are CNAMEd to Tradedoubler's infrastructure via DNS records.

DNS Setup Process:
1. Client provides their root domain
2. Tradedoubler tech team configures the tracking infrastructure
3. Client receives DNS values to implement:
   - visit.domain → CNAME to imp.tradedoubler.com
   - statics.domain → CNAME to statics.tradedoubler.com
4. DNS propagation takes 24-48 hours
5. Once live, tracking scripts use the branded domains

Is It Optional?
No, branded tracking is now the standard implementation for all new programs. It's essential for tracking accuracy in modern browsers.",
    metadata: {
      source: "branded-tracking-guide",
      category: "setup",
      topic: "branded-tracking",
      subtopic: "dns-configuration",
      stage: "onboarding",
      difficulty: "beginner",
      keywords: ["branded tracking", "DNS", "CNAME", "subdomain", "first-party", "Safari ITP", "visit domain", "statics domain"],
      related_topics: ["dns-setup", "first-party-tracking", "cookies"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "DNS CONFIGURATION DETAILED GUIDE

Step-by-Step DNS Setup:
1. Log into your DNS provider (GoDaddy, Cloudflare, etc.)
2. Navigate to DNS Management
3. Add two CNAME records:

Record 1:
- Type: CNAME
- Host: visit
- Points to: imp.tradedoubler.com
- TTL: 3600 (or default)

Record 2:
- Type: CNAME
- Host: statics
- Points to: statics.tradedoubler.com
- TTL: 3600

Common DNS Providers:
- Cloudflare: Fast propagation (minutes to hours)
- GoDaddy: 24-48 hours
- AWS Route 53: Very fast propagation

Troubleshooting:
- Check DNS propagation: whatsmydns.net
- Ensure no conflicting A records
- Some providers don't support root CNAME
- Allow 24-48 hours for full propagation",
    metadata: {
      source: "dns-configuration-guide",
      category: "setup",
      topic: "dns-setup",
      subtopic: "cname-records",
      stage: "onboarding",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["DNS", "CNAME", "DNS propagation", "whatsmydns", "Cloudflare", "GoDaddy", "DNS provider"],
      related_topics: ["branded-tracking", "troubleshooting"]
    }
  },

  // -------------------------
  // TRACKING IMPLEMENTATION
  // -------------------------
  {
    pageContent: "TRACKING IMPLEMENTATION TECHNICAL GUIDE

Tracking Library Overview:
The Tradedoubler tracking library (trsdk) must be implemented on ALL pages of your website, not just confirmation pages. It doesn't track anything until explicitly told to fire a conversion event.

Two Required Scripts:

1. LANDING PAGE SCRIPT (All Pages):
- Sets the first-party TDUID cookie
- Captures click parameters from affiliate links
- Stores session data
- Must be present for any tracking to work

2. CONFIRMATION PAGE SCRIPT (Thank You Page Only):
- Fires the actual conversion event
- Sends transaction details (order ID, value, currency)
- Credits the publisher who referred the sale

Best Practices:
- Place scripts directly in HTML rather than GTM
- Landing script loads first
- Always test with TD test links before going live

Required Data Layer Parameters:
- orderID
- orderValue (numeric)
- currency (ISO)
- voucher (optional)
- products (optional)",
    metadata: {
      source: "tracking-implementation",
      category: "implementation",
      topic: "tracking-scripts",
      subtopic: "script-placement",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["tracking script", "trsdk", "landing page script", "confirmation script", "TDUID", "implementation"],
      related_topics: ["confirmation-script", "gtm-implementation", "testing"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "LANDING PAGE SCRIPT GUIDE

Purpose: The landing page script must be on every page of your website to set the TDUID cookie when users click affiliate links.

Implementation:
<script src="https://[your-statics-subdomain]/trsdk" async></script>

What it does:
- Reads click parameters from URL
- Sets first-party TDUID cookie
- Stores publisher/campaign information
- Does NOT fire any conversion events

Placement:
- Before </body> tag
- On all pages (home, product, cart, checkout, confirmation)
- Can be async loaded

Common Mistakes:
- Only adding to confirmation page (won't work!)
- Forgetting async attribute
- Using wrong subdomain URL",
    metadata: {
      source: "landing-page-script",
      category: "implementation",
      topic: "tracking-scripts",
      subtopic: "landing-page",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "beginner",
      keywords: ["landing page script", "trsdk", "TDUID cookie", "all pages", "async"],
      related_topics: ["tracking-scripts", "cookies", "implementation"]
    }
  },

  // -------------------------
  // CONFIRMATION SCRIPT
  // -------------------------
  {
    pageContent: "CONFIRMATION PAGE SCRIPT — FULL EXAMPLE & EXPLANATION

This script must run on the thank-you page after a purchase.

<script>
var TDConversion = {
  transactionId: '[ORDER_NUMBER]',
  ordervalue: '[ORDER_VALUE]',
  currency: '[CURRENCY]',
  event: EventID,
  voucher: '[VOUCHER]',
  cdt: { extId: '[EXITID]', extType: 1 },
  validOn: '[VALID_ON]',
  products: '[PRODUCT_DATA]'
};
tdconv('track', 'sale', TDConversion);
tdfallback('https://a.imgstatic.eu/trsdk');
</script>

Required:
- transactionId unique
- ordervalue numeric
- currency ISO code
- event ID from TD

Optional:
- voucher
- products
- extId/extType
- validOn",
    metadata: {
      source: "confirmation-script-guide",
      category: "implementation",
      topic: "confirmation-script",
      subtopic: "basic-implementation",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["confirmation script", "TDConversion", "thank you page", "ordervalue", "transactionId", "currency", "tdconv"],
      related_topics: ["tracking-scripts", "data-layer", "gtm-implementation"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "CONFIRMATION SCRIPT FIELD DEFINITIONS

transactionId:
- Unique order number.

ordervalue:
- Numeric only.

currency:
- Must be ISO code.

event:
- TD Event ID.

voucher:
- Discount code.

cdt.extId:
- External ID.

validOn:
- For delayed validation.

products:
- JSON array of purchased items.",
    metadata: {
      source: "confirmation-script-guide",
      category: "implementation",
      topic: "confirmation-script",
      subtopic: "field-definitions",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "beginner",
      keywords: ["transactionId", "ordervalue", "currency", "event", "voucher", "products", "field definitions"],
      related_topics: ["confirmation-script", "data-validation"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "PRODUCT DATA FORMATTING

products: [
  { id: 'SKU001', name: 'Blue T-shirt', price: 19.99, quantity: 2 },
  { id: 'SKU002', name: 'Jeans', price: 49.99, quantity: 1 }
]

Rules:
- Must be valid JSON
- Prices numeric
- IDs should match product feed
- Quantity recommended",
    metadata: {
      source: "confirmation-script-guide",
      category: "implementation",
      topic: "confirmation-script",
      subtopic: "product-data",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["products", "JSON", "SKU", "price", "quantity", "product data"],
      related_topics: ["confirmation-script", "product-feed", "plt"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "DATA LAYER → CONFIRMATION SCRIPT MAPPING

Example dataLayer:
window.dataLayer.push({
  orderID: '12345',
  orderValue: 89.99,
  currency: 'GBP',
  voucher: 'SUMMER20',
  products: [...]
});

Mapping:
transactionId: dataLayer.orderID
ordervalue: dataLayer.orderValue
currency: dataLayer.currency
voucher: dataLayer.voucher
products: dataLayer.products",
    metadata: {
      source: "confirmation-script-guide",
      category: "implementation",
      topic: "confirmation-script",
      subtopic: "data-layer-mapping",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["dataLayer", "mapping", "orderID", "orderValue", "currency", "data layer"],
      related_topics: ["confirmation-script", "gtm-implementation"]
    }
  },

  // -------------------------
  // GTM IMPLEMENTATION
  // -------------------------
  {
    pageContent: "GOOGLE TAG MANAGER IMPLEMENTATION GUIDE

Landing Page Tag:
- Custom HTML
- Trigger: All Pages
- Must fire before other tags

Confirmation Page Tag:
- Custom HTML
- Trigger: Thank-you page
- Requires orderID not empty

GTM Data Layer Variables:
- orderID
- orderValue
- currency
- voucher

Testing Steps:
- Use Preview Mode
- Click Tradedoubler test link
- Complete test purchase
- Verify both tags fired
- Check confirmation tag received correct values",
    metadata: {
      source: "gtm-guide",
      category: "implementation",
      topic: "gtm-implementation",
      subtopic: "tag-setup",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["GTM", "Google Tag Manager", "Custom HTML", "trigger", "dataLayer", "tag"],
      related_topics: ["tracking-scripts", "gtm-testing", "data-layer"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "GTM CONFIRMATION SCRIPT IMPLEMENTATION

Custom HTML Tag:
<script>
var dl = window.dataLayer[0] || {};
var TDConversion = {
  transactionId: dl.orderID,
  ordervalue: dl.orderValue,
  currency: dl.currency,
  voucher: dl.voucher,
  products: dl.products
};
tdconv('track', 'sale', TDConversion);
tdfallback('https://a.imgstatic.eu/trsdk');
</script>

Best Practices:
- Fire after dataLayer push
- Avoid duplicate triggers
- Use Preview Mode",
    metadata: {
      source: "confirmation-script-guide",
      category: "implementation",
      topic: "gtm-implementation",
      subtopic: "confirmation-tag",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["GTM", "confirmation tag", "Custom HTML", "dataLayer", "TDConversion"],
      related_topics: ["confirmation-script", "gtm-implementation", "data-layer"]
    }
  },

  // -------------------------
  // TESTING - CRITICAL SECTION
  // -------------------------
  {
    pageContent: "IMPORTANT NOTE ABOUT GTM PREVIEW MODE FOR TRADEDOUBLER TESTING

Google Tag Manager Preview Mode is useful for checking:
- The confirmation tag fires
- The landing page tag fires
- DataLayer variables populate correctly

BUT it cannot be used to perform a real Tradedoubler tracking test.

WHY PREVIEW MODE DOES NOT WORK FOR TD TESTING:
- Tradedoubler test click links do NOT route through GTM Preview Mode.
- The preview container URL wrapper interferes with the redirect.
- The tracking pixel request will NOT reach Tradedoubler servers.

CORRECT TESTING PROCEDURE:
1. Exit GTM Preview Mode completely.
2. Click the official Tradedoubler test link provided.
3. Complete a real test transaction on the live thank you page.
4. Check that the conversion appears in Tradedoubler systems (10–45 mins).

USAGE GUIDELINES:
- Use GTM Preview Mode ONLY to verify tag firing sequencing.
- Use REAL TEST LINKS for actual tracking validation.",
    metadata: {
      source: "gtm-testing-guidelines",
      category: "testing",
      topic: "gtm-testing",
      subtopic: "preview-mode-limitations",
      stage: "testing",
      difficulty: "beginner",
      keywords: ["GTM Preview Mode", "testing", "test link", "limitations", "cannot test", "preview mode does not work", "exit preview mode"],
      related_topics: ["gtm-implementation", "testing", "troubleshooting"],
      critical: true  // Flag for high-importance content
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "TESTING & TROUBLESHOOTING GUIDE

Checklist:
- Clear cookies/cache
- Disable ad blockers

Session Test:
- Click TD test link
- Purchase immediately

Cookie Test:
- Click TD link
- Close browser
- Return later and purchase

If tracking fails:
- Check cookie set
- Check scripts present
- Check network requests
- Check values numeric

Browser Notes:
- Safari requires branded tracking
- Test across devices",
    metadata: {
      source: "testing-guide",
      category: "testing",
      topic: "testing-procedures",
      subtopic: "test-checklist",
      stage: "testing",
      difficulty: "beginner",
      keywords: ["testing", "test link", "cookies", "troubleshooting", "verification", "session test"],
      related_topics: ["troubleshooting", "gtm-testing", "cookies"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "CONFIRMATION SCRIPT TROUBLESHOOTING GUIDE

Common Issues:
- Script not firing
- Undefined dataLayer values
- Wrong currency format
- No TDUID cookie
- Duplicate conversions
- DNS not propagated for branded domains

Diagnostic Steps:
- Check Network tab
- console.log(TDConversion)
- Validate event ID
- Confirm values numeric",
    metadata: {
      source: "confirmation-script-guide",
      category: "troubleshooting",
      topic: "confirmation-script-issues",
      subtopic: "common-errors",
      stage: "troubleshooting",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["troubleshooting", "script not firing", "undefined values", "TDUID missing", "duplicate conversions", "network tab"],
      related_topics: ["confirmation-script", "testing", "cookies"]
    }
  },

  // -------------------------
  // COOKIES & FIRST-PARTY
  // -------------------------
  {
    pageContent: "FIRST-PARTY TRACKING & COOKIES DEEP DIVE

Why it matters:
- Safari ITP blocks 3rd-party cookies
- First-party cookies last full 365 days

Flow:
1. User clicks affiliate link → visit.domain
2. TDUID cookie is set first-party
3. user lands on advertiser site
4. Conversion reads TDUID

Advantages:
- Better accuracy
- Works in ITP browsers
- Fully compliant",
    metadata: {
      source: "first-party-tracking",
      category: "technical-concepts",
      topic: "cookies",
      subtopic: "first-party-cookies",
      stage: "onboarding",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["first-party", "cookies", "TDUID", "Safari ITP", "third-party", "cookie lifetime"],
      related_topics: ["branded-tracking", "consent-management"]
    }
  },

  // -------------------------
  // CONSENT & COMPLIANCE
  // -------------------------
  {
    pageContent: "CONSENT MANAGEMENT & GDPR COMPLIANCE

tdconv('tracking-consent', true/false)

Consent = TRUE:
- Full tracking enabled
- TDUID cookie created

Consent = FALSE:
- No cookie stored
- No user tracking

Recommended CMP Flow:
1. Load landing script under Strictly Necessary
2. Set tdconv('tracking-consent', false)
3. On accept → tdconv('tracking-consent', true)
4. On reject → remain false

Supports OneTrust, Cookiebot, Usercentrics.",
    metadata: {
      source: "consent-management",
      category: "compliance",
      topic: "consent",
      subtopic: "gdpr",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["consent", "GDPR", "tracking-consent", "CMP", "OneTrust", "Cookiebot", "privacy"],
      related_topics: ["cookies", "compliance"]
    }
  },

  // -------------------------
  // PRODUCT FEED
  // -------------------------
  {
    pageContent: "MANDATORY PRODUCT FIELDS (XML & CSV)

Required:
- productFeed version="2.0"
- product id (max 50 chars)
- name
- description (max 2990)
- price (numeric)
- productURL
- imageURL
- category

Missing any mandatory field → product dropped.",
    metadata: {
      source: "product-feed-formats-pdf",
      category: "product-feed",
      topic: "feed-structure",
      subtopic: "mandatory-fields",
      stage: "onboarding",
      difficulty: "beginner",
      keywords: ["product feed", "mandatory fields", "required", "XML", "CSV", "product id", "price", "productURL"],
      related_topics: ["optional-fields", "xml-rules", "csv-rules"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "OPTIONAL PRODUCT FIELDS

Optional:
- availability
- brand
- condition
- deliveryTime
- ean / upc / isbn
- inStock
- previousPrice
- promoText
- shortDescription
- shippingCost
- sku
- size / weight
- techSpecs
- tdCategoryId
- <field name/value> pairs",
    metadata: {
      source: "product-feed-formats-pdf",
      category: "product-feed",
      topic: "feed-structure",
      subtopic: "optional-fields",
      stage: "onboarding",
      difficulty: "beginner",
      keywords: ["product feed", "optional fields", "brand", "availability", "sku", "ean", "shippingCost"],
      related_topics: ["mandatory-fields", "feed-optimization"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "FILE SIZE LIMITS & FEED IMPORT

Recommended:
- ≤100MB
- ≤100k products

Absolute Max:
- 256MB uncompressed

Import Frequency:
- Every ~24 hours

Feed Requirements:
- Single URL
- Credentials included if needed

Product ID rules:
- Must remain consistent.",
    metadata: {
      source: "product-feed-formats-pdf",
      category: "product-feed",
      topic: "feed-management",
      subtopic: "file-limits",
      stage: "onboarding",
      difficulty: "beginner",
      keywords: ["file size", "limits", "100MB", "import frequency", "product ID", "feed URL"],
      related_topics: ["feed-optimization", "troubleshooting"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "XML V2 RULES

- XML is case-sensitive
- Must include XML prologue
- Allowed entities: & < > ' "
- No HTML entities allowed
- CDATA cannot be used inside attributes
- Encoding mismatches cause strange characters",
    metadata: {
      source: "product-feed-formats-pdf",
      category: "product-feed",
      topic: "feed-formatting",
      subtopic: "xml-rules",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "intermediate",
      keywords: ["XML", "case-sensitive", "XML prologue", "entities", "CDATA", "encoding"],
      related_topics: ["mandatory-fields", "feed-troubleshooting"]
    }
  },

  {
    pageContent: "CSV RULES

Allowed separators: ; , |
One product per line.
Use text qualifiers when separator appears in text.
CSV does not decode HTML entities.
Headers require 'Ignore First Line' enabled.",
    metadata: {
      source: "product-feed-formats-pdf",
      category: "product-feed",
      topic: "feed-formatting",
      subtopic: "csv-rules",
      stage: "implementation",
      difficulty: "beginner",
      keywords: ["CSV", "separator", "delimiter", "text qualifier", "headers", "ignore first line"],
      related_topics: ["mandatory-fields", "feed-troubleshooting"]
    }
  }

];

return documents.map(doc => ({ json: doc }));
